name: Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship (Poland)

colors.symbolism: the central field bearing the regional arms
the hoist stripe, drawn from the regional heraldry

description: A yellow vertical stripe at the hoist, a central white panel charged with the regional coat of arms, and narrow blue and red horizontal bars along the top and bottom of the white field.
history: Adopted by the Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship after its formation in 1999, taking the colours of the historic Sandomierz and Holy Cross territories.

features.symbolism: Compound layout of a yellow hoist stripe, a central white panel bearing the regional arms, and blue and red bars at top and bottom — combining the heraldic colours of the historic territories united in the voivodeship.
represents the Holy Cross region and its historic constituents
